title: commelina radicans
intro: Commelina radicans, also known as creeping dayflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Commelinaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Commelina radicans include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
